facts.text: Hollis developed as one of Queens’ early railroad suburbs and later became a center of Black middle‑class homeownership, reflecting stability and self‑determination.
The Liberty Rock, erected in 1928 as a World War I memorial, now symbolizes the African‑American community’s history and culture in Hollis.
Hollis’s boundaries are defined by Hillside Ave north, Francis Lewis Blvd east, Hollis Ave and 104th Ave south, and 180th St west, giving it a distinct suburban feel.
Hollis’s cultural tapestry blends African‑American heritage with Caribbean and other immigrant influences, creating a vibrant, diverse community.
In the 1920s Hollis attracted a wave of talent and resilience, laying the groundwork for its strong community bonds and vibrant cultural scene.
